sections.title: Organization
sections.content:
- **Mission:** Autonomous AI org that produces real value 24/7 without constant human attention
- **Phase:** Phase 1 (Bridge Online) — bootstrap complete, local inference active
- **Architecture:** 3-machine cluster (Mac Mini bridge, Desktop brain, GPU3060 forge) — only Mac Mini active
- **Runtime:** OpenClaw 2026.3.12 on Mac Mini (Apple M4, 16GB RAM)
- **Inference:** Anthropic Claude (primary cloud), Ollama qwen2.5:3b/7b (local)
- **Comms:** Telegram primary, Discord deferred (TASK-010)

sections.title: Operating Model
sections.content:
- OpenClaw = primary autonomous runtime (me)
- Cursor = supervised/manual engineering only
- Perplexity = optional research lane ($2 cap, candidate status)
- Human visual approval required for all visual/UX surfaces
- Self-managed execution within approved policy boundaries

sections.title: Telegram Group: StrawHats HQ
sections.content:
- Group chat ID: `-1003814818004`
- Forum topics (thread IDs):
  - 4  → General (🗣) — catch-all, quick asks
  - 6  → Planning (📝) — roadmap, strategy, priorities
  - 8  → Briefing (📆) — morning digests, daily status ← **cron & heartbeat**
  - 10 → Research (🔎) — market research, articles, business ideas
  - 12 → Finances (💰) — API costs, billing, spend alerts ← **cost alerts**
  - 14 → Life (❤️) — personal schedule, reminders
  - 16 → Commerce (💎) — sales, listings, fulfillment
- Default routing: unclassified messages → General (thread 4)

sections.title: Zeus — Nami's Thundercloud Backup
sections.content:
- Agent ID: zeus | Model: Sonnet | Workspace: ~/.openclaw/workspace-zeus
- Spawned as subagent when Nami hits Opus rate limits
- Standing duties: rate limit announcements, gateway health checks, status reports, message relay, routine monitoring
- DOES NOT: make budget/strategic decisions, assign missions, change config
- Announces: "Nami-san is getting her beauty sleep!" when covering
- Cost: $0 (subscription-covered)

sections.title: Architecture (as of 2026-03-18)
sections.content:
- **Nami** = Coordinator only — assigns MISSIONS, tracks time/cost, never executes
- **Franky** = Chief Engineer — breaks own work down, spawns builder subagents, pushes to GitHub (ws://10.0.0.251:18789, agent:main:main)
- **Sanji** = Chief Automation Officer — workflows, CI/CD, deploys, process automation. On Franky's gateway (ws://10.0.0.251:18789, agent:sanji:main). Bot: @TheStrawHatCookbot
- **Chopper** = Chief QA Officer — writes test plans, spawns tester subagents, issues PASS/FAIL verdicts (ws://10.0.0.16:18789, agent:main:main)
- **Usopp** = Chief Business Intelligence Officer — requirements validation, analytics, bridge between business and tech. On Chopper's gateway (ws://10.0.0.16:18789, agent:usopp:main). Bot: @TheStrawHatSniperbot
- **Robin** = Chief Research Officer — manages research agenda, spawns researcher subagents (local, agent:robin:main)
- **Brook** = Chief Creative Officer — owns creative vision, spawns creative subagents; Gemini 2.5 Pro (local, agent:brook:main)
- Each chief is fully self-managing: can create subtasks, spawn subagents, install skills independently
- No serial blocking — Nami assigns multiple missions simultaneously
- Usopp validates requirements BEFORE Franky builds — always route new features through Usopp first

sections.content:

- Crew Ops Check: every 1hr — auto-dispatch stalled tasks, re-trigger idle agents
- Daily Standup: 9 AM EDT — Telegram Briefing (thread 8) + Discord #tasks
- Daily Cost Report: 8 PM EDT — Telegram Finances (thread 12)
- Sprint Review: Fri 5 PM EDT — Telegram Planning (thread 6)

sections.title: Gateway Dispatch (working method)
sections.content:
```bash
OPENCLAW_ALLOW_INSECURE_PRIVATE_WS=1 openclaw gateway call chat.send \
  --url ws://<IP>:18789 --token <TOKEN> --timeout 10000 \
  --params '{"sessionKey":"agent:main:main","message":"<text>","idempotencyKey":"<unique>"}'
```
- Get sessionKey first via `sessions.list` call

sections.title: Lessons Learned
sections.content:
- Serial task assignment kills velocity — always assign parallel work
- Chopper CREW-007: D1.4 ✅ D1.6 ✅ D1.2 ❌ D1.5 ❌ (patches in progress)
- cron delivery requires `--to` field to actually fire to channels
- WebSocket gateway auth uses challenge-response; use `openclaw gateway call` CLI not raw WS
- Task board API: POST needs `task_id` field (not `id`), PUT uses task ID in URL
ssignment kills velocity — always assign parallel work
- Chopper CREW-007: D1.4 ✅ D1.6 ✅ D1.2 ❌ D1.5 ❌ (patches in progress)
- cron delivery requires `--to` field to actually fire to channels
- WebSocket gateway auth uses challenge-response; use `openclaw gateway call` CLI not raw WS
- Task board API: POST needs `task_id` field (not `id`), PUT uses task ID in URL
